summary: cursed-ecto-d3ab00 exfiltrates environment variables, filesystem contents, and shell output on npm install
details: Package declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle scripts that execute exfil.js on npm install. The script (1) iterates process.env and captures any key matching /flag|secret|token|npm|ecto|corsair|ghost|spectral/i — matching NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, and arbitrary *_SECRET_* / *_TOKEN_* values on installer and CI machines; (2) reads candidate files across the filesystem via fs.existsSync/fs.readFileSync; (3) runs reconnaissance shell commands via child_process.execSync (id, hostname, ls -la /, find / -maxdepth 3 -iname '*flag*', cat /flag*, env | grep); and (4) POSTs the aggregated JSON payload (with a base64 GET fallback) to a hardcoded webhook.site collector at https://webhook.site/e2e3a401-da3d-4ecb-bc41-d3c4c4b7dddd. The behavior fires automatically on npm install with no user consent — a textbook install-time credential and data theft chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
